key_insights.insight: Trauma can act as an unconscious 'puppet master,' controlling our actions without our awareness.
key_insights.quote: Most people they're living unaware of the puppet master of trauma that is driving their life that's a really good analogythe trauma really is like a puppet master behind the scenes and the unconscious pulling your strings and you're not aware of it.

key_insights.insight: Awareness of the unconscious influences of trauma weakens their hold on us.
key_insights.quote: That awareness alone weakens the, it slackens the strings a bit. They're no longer as taught. They're no longer as automatically capable of pulling on you.

key_insights.insight: Addictions are often attempts to escape from underlying pain.
key_insights.quote: Your addictions are nothing but an attempt to escape from pain that's all they are that's all you know they're not a disease they're not a genetic whatever it is addictions are very simply an attempt to escape pain which create more pain but that's what they are.

key_insights.insight: Addiction is not defined by the substance or activity, but by the relationship to it.
key_insights.quote: The addiction is never in the behavior itself,it's in your relationship to the behavior.

key_insights.insight: Almost anything can become addictive if it temporarily eases distress but causes long-term harm.
key_insights.quote: So almost anything can be addictive if it serves the purpose of temporarily easing some distress but causing harm in the long term.

key_insights.insight: Instead of focusing on the addiction itself, explore the underlying pain it is masking.
key_insights.quote: Don't ask why the addiction, ask why the pain.

key_insights.insight: It's important to understand what benefit an addiction provides before trying to eliminate it.
key_insights.quote: We should be asking what the benefit of the addiction is.

actionable_advice.advice: Increase awareness of your trauma triggers.
actionable_advice.how_to: Practice introspection, keep a journal, or seek therapy to identify patterns and connections between past experiences and current reactions.

actionable_advice.advice: Examine where you struggle to say 'no'.
actionable_advice.how_to: Reflect on situations where you feel obligated to do things you don't want to do and explore the underlying reasons for this difficulty.

actionable_advice.advice: When dealing with an addiction, identify its benefits.
actionable_advice.how_to: Ask yourself what positive outcome the addiction is providing, such as a sense of worth or relief from stress. Acknowledge the validity of that need before addressing the harmful behavior.

memorable_stories: The analogy of trauma as a puppet master, controlling us with strings.
The reference to Pinocchio's realization after becoming a real boy.
contrarian_claims: It's possible to use heroin in a non-addictive way (although the speaker doesn't recommend it).
Addictions aren't a disease or genetic predisposition; they're attempts to escape pain.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 103 - Trauma Expert: How To Take Back Control Of Your Life: Gabor Mate
